name: CO2 emissions
source: Global Carbon Budget via Our World in Data
explainer: Carbon dioxide released each year from burning fossil fuels and industry. The main driver of global warming.

name: CO2 emissions per person
source: Global Carbon Budget via Our World in Data
explainer: Each person's share of their country's yearly CO2 emissions. A fairer way to compare countries of very different sizes.

name: Temperature anomaly
source: Copernicus Climate Change Service (ERA5) via Our World in Data
explainer: How much warmer or cooler a country's year was compared with its 1991-2020 average. Watch the map turn red as you move the slider towards today.

name: Renewable energy share
source: Energy Institute Statistical Review via Our World in Data
explainer: The slice of a country's energy that comes from renewables such as wind, solar and hydro.

name: Fossil fuel share
source: Energy Institute Statistical Review via Our World in Data
explainer: The slice of a country's energy still coming from coal, oil and gas. The number the world is trying to shrink.

name: Energy use per person
source: Energy Institute / Ember via Our World in Data
explainer: How much energy each person uses in a year, across electricity, transport, heating and industry.

name: Methane emissions
source: Global Carbon Project / PRIMAP via Our World in Data
explainer: Methane traps around 80 times more heat than CO2 over 20 years. It comes mainly from livestock, rice, landfill and leaky fossil fuel infrastructure.

name: Tree cover loss
source: Global Forest Watch (Hansen/UMD) via Our World in Data
explainer: Forest area lost each year to fire, logging, farming and storms. Not all loss is permanent deforestation, but the trend matters.

name: Forest area
source: FAO via Our World in Data
explainer: How much of a country's land is covered by forest.

name: Deaths from natural disasters
source: IHME Global Burden of Disease via Our World in Data
explainer: People killed by floods, storms, droughts, heatwaves, earthquakes and other disasters each year. Single events dominate single years.

name: Global sea level
source: Church & White / UHSLC via Our World in Data
explainer: The global average sea surface height. It rises as warming water expands and land ice melts into the ocean.

name: Ocean heat content (top 2000m)
source: NOAA NCEI via Our World in Data
explainer: Over 90% of the extra heat trapped by greenhouse gases ends up in the ocean. This is the planet's clearest warming signal.

name: Ice sheet mass change
source: NASA GRACE via Our World in Data
explainer: How much ice Greenland and Antarctica have lost since 2002, measured from space by tracking tiny changes in Earth's gravity.

name: Water stress
source: FAO AQUASTAT (SDG 6.4.2) via Our World in Data
explainer: How much of a country's renewable freshwater is withdrawn each year. Above 25% counts as stressed; above 100% means drawing down reserves.

name: Renewable water per person
source: FAO AQUASTAT via Our World in Data
explainer: Each person's share of the country's renewable freshwater. Below 1,700 m³ is water stress; below 1,000 m³ is scarcity.

name: Safely managed drinking water
source: WHO/UNICEF JMP via Our World in Data
explainer: The share of people with safe drinking water at home, available when needed and free from contamination.

name: Annual precipitation
source: Copernicus Climate Change Service (ERA5) via Our World in Data
explainer: Total rain and snow that fell over a country in a year, averaged across its land area.

name: Rainfall anomaly
source: Derived from Copernicus ERA5 precipitation via Our World in Data
explainer: How much wetter (blue) or drier (red) a country's year was compared with its 1961-1990 average. A simple drought and deluge signal.

name: Air pollution (PM2.5)
source: World Bank / van Donkelaar et al. via Our World in Data
explainer: Average exposure to fine particles small enough to enter the bloodstream. The WHO guideline is 5 µg/m³.
